ultimate_goal: Build a movement connecting stagehands with training, jobs, and community through an integrated ecosystem of labor management, skill development, and opportunity discovery.
phase_description: Establish the core labor sourcing and training infrastructure to create a foundation for long-term community building.

this_week_focus.note: Finalize/de-duplicate the Deadhang business documents (dh-5) and close out taxes (dh-8); get the contractor agreement + MSA attorney-reviewed (dh-10); keep advancing Crew Blueprint content and Production Atlas research.

branches.name: Deadhang Labor LLC
branches.role: Solo contractor turning into labor staffing agency
branches.ultimate_goal: Establish Deadhang as a reliable labor sourcing and staffing entity that can contract large gigs and deploy teams of stagehands.
branches.current_state: Operating as an Arizona single-member LLC (Schedule C, cash basis, owner-operated) in live-event production labor. Foundation is largely in place: EIN, business bank account, website (live + SSL, with complete privacy/cookie/terms pages), business email, invoice system + a live gig/contracts/payments tracker, W-9 workflow, record retention, a Client MSA template, a drafted Master Independent Contractor Agreement, a City of Mesa business license, and full canonical documentation (companies/deadhang-labor/ — incl. a living current-state business plan, a document register, and SOPs). In progress: finalizing/de-duplicating the business-document set (dh-5), closing out taxes (dh-8), and safety documentation (dh-11). The IC agreement's remaining gate is Arizona attorney review (dh-10). Insurance is intentionally deferred until scaling (dh-9). Still to build: team-deployment capability (dh-3/dh-4). A print-on-demand merch line is being added (dh-12, no new LLC).
branches.critical_blocker: Independent-contractor agreement drafted — Arizona attorney review is the gate before engaging others; safety documentation and team-deployment capability still to build (insurance deferred until scaling).

timeline.description: Validate business structure, establish team deployment framework, build initial contractor network

blockers: Independent-contractor agreement drafted — Arizona attorney review pending before operational use
Safety documentation in progress
Team-deployment capability not yet built
Insurance deferred until the appropriate scaling stage
Business documents need finalization: de-duplicate to one canonical version each and consolidate account/folder ownership

branches.name: The Crew Blueprint
branches.role: Skill development and industry knowledge resource
branches.ultimate_goal: Provide stagehands with a visual, workflow-based learning system that teaches skills, context, and industry integration without formal certification.
branches.current_state: Foundation complete and no longer blocked by software development. Vision, standardized course architecture, multi-AI content workflow, brand, and legal/authority philosophy (job-readiness, not certification) are all decided. Website shell is live (WordPress.com, industrial Extendable theme, staging-first, Coming Soon) and the modular plugin architecture is defined. The next-generation platform is intentionally deferred; the website stays a lightweight presentation layer. Primary work is now content and documentation: produce canonical course packets, continue Stagehand Fundamentals, and organize the discipline/pathway library. Canonical documentation lives in companies/crew-blueprint/.
branches.critical_blocker: Not platform anymore — the constraint is content-production volume: producing canonical course packets and the discipline/pathway library.

timeline.description: Complete resource library organization by discipline/pathway, launch foundational curriculum

blockers: Content-production volume is the real constraint (packets take time)
Discipline/pathway repository mapping still to be built out
Next-generation platform (LMS/Laravel/AWS/etc.) intentionally deferred until content justifies it

work_items.why: Establish trust framework before scaling training content deployment

work_items.task: Map discipline/pathway repository structure
work_items.why: Organize content logically for learner navigation across roles and skill levels

work_items.task: Stabilize/build next-generation platform infrastructure
work_items.why: Deferred by decision — keep the website lightweight until content volume justifies building the next platform

work_items.task: Create foundational discipline resources (canonical course packets)
work_items.why: Build initial content library for core stagehand skills; content is now the primary work

branches.name: Production Atlas
branches.role: Live-event industry intelligence & opportunity-research platform (not a job board)
branches.ultimate_goal: Become the U.S. live-event industry's primary research platform — making employer discovery, vendor ecosystems, career pathways, opportunity mapping, and regional production intelligence structured, searchable, and actionable.
branches.current_state: Moved beyond defining its architecture. It is a static GitHub Pages app (no backend/login/database/payment/scraping) that reads manifest-driven, structured research packages; product is 'Production Atlas' in the festival-atlas repo, with research on the research-version branch. Settled: vision, architecture, branching, research methodology, public-safe data model, and validation. Application navigation (~11 pages) and research framework are ~95%. Department research ~85% complete (Staging, Rigging, Lighting, Audio, Video/LED, Power, Site Ops, Logistics substantially done; Scenic started, not finished). Remaining: complete Scenic, expand employer/opportunity datasets, finish normalized-date wiring, usability, and governance/docs. Canonical documentation lives in companies/production-atlas/.
branches.critical_blocker: Not architecture anymore — remaining work is research-corpus depth (Scenic + datasets), usability, and governance/documentation.

timeline.description: Complete the research corpus (Scenic + datasets), improve usability, and establish governance so the repo is a durable collaborative knowledge system

blockers: Scenic department research incomplete
Employer/opportunity datasets still expanding
Normalized date system not yet wired into all applicable pages
Mobile usability, filtering, and planning views need improvement
Governance & collaboration documentation still light (~40%)

work_items.task: Improve usability (mobile, filtering, planning views) and finish normalized-date wiring
work_items.why: Make the mature app easier to use across pages; the interface exists and now needs polish

branches.name: Contractor Tools
branches.role: Infrastructure for payments, scheduling, and team coordination
branches.ultimate_goal: Provide contractors with integrated tools for invoicing, payment tracking, calendar management, and team communication.
branches.current_state: Shared infrastructure layer for contractors. Built and in place: a production-ready 1099 invoice generator (single-file HTML, deterministic parse→review→PDF pipeline, multiple payment terms) and a finance/payment-tracking system. Calendar infrastructure works via ICS subscription feeds (Google/Apple/Outlook). Remaining: reliable calendar authorization renewal, and validating backend scope before overbuilding (MVP-first discipline). Canonical documentation lives in companies/contractor-tools/.
branches.critical_blocker: Invoice and calendar systems need validation against actual MVP requirements

branches.name: Personal Operations / Roadmap System
branches.role: Decision framework and execution system for the entire initiative
branches.ultimate_goal: Maintain a living, operational roadmap system that guides decisions, tracks progress, prevents sprawl, and communicates vision to stakeholders.
branches.current_state: Mature and operational. The command center is live on GitHub Pages with roadmap.json (branch model) as the single source of truth, rendered as Timeline + Dashboard + 30/60/90 + branch hubs + The Vision views (professional palette, pagination). Governance framework is established (AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG.md, governance/, DATA_SAFETY_POLICY.md, public-safety schema, and a CI validator). The AI pipeline is built (process-memories, watcher, prioritizer, validator — proposals stay human-in-the-loop). Canonical company documentation is being built out under companies/. Remaining: finish anti-sprawl scope gates, downloadable documentation templates, and the eventual database migration (deferred, OD-5). Canonical documentation lives in companies/personal-operations/.
branches.critical_blocker: Not blocked — remaining work is polish (anti-sprawl scope gates, documentation templates) and the deferred database migration (OD-5).

branches.name: The Homestead / Homes for Hands
branches.role: The destination - a community home base for traveling stagehands
branches.ultimate_goal: A centralized place where stagehands and traveling workers rest between gigs, find resources, get trained, and connect with contractors to source work - staying for a nominal fee, with a path for permanent residents who help care for the community.
branches.current_state: Vision stage and the ultimate destination. Deferred until the Phase 1 engine runs and land is secured. Housing is added later, on top of the labor / training / job-placement flow.
branches.critical_blocker: Requires the Phase 1 foundation and acquired land before it can begin

timeline.description: Begins after the engine is self-sustaining and land is secured

blockers: Depends on the Phase 1 Labor to Training to Jobs engine
Depends on Land Acquisition
Operating model (stays, fees, resident stewardship) not yet defined

work_items.why: Turn the vision into concrete rules for how the place runs

work_items.why: This is the core service the Homestead provides between gigs

work_items.task: Define the permanent-resident / community-care standard
work_items.why: Set expectations for people who stay and help keep the community running

work_items.task: Investigate & connect with nomadic-movement organizations (rated list in connections.md)
work_items.why: Build relationships with orgs that support traveling/live-event workers and community/land models for the Homestead. Top picks: The Roadie Clinic, Backline, Workamper News, Van Life Campgrounds. Full rated shortlist: data/roadmap/connections.md

flow: 1. Deadhang Labor LLC secures contracts and generates revenue
2. Production Atlas discovers and surfaces job opportunities
3. The Crew Blueprint trains stagehands in the required skills
4. Contractor Tools manage scheduling, communication, and payments
5. Contractors complete gigs through Deadhang deployment
6. Repeat: System learns from gigs, improves training, expands opportunities

north_star: Build the movement and the Homestead: a community home base where traveling stagehands rest between gigs, find resources, get trained, and connect to work — powered by a self-sustaining Labor to Training to Jobs engine.

end_goal.destination: The Homestead + the movement around it
end_goal.what_it_is: A centralized place where stagehands and traveling workers stop and rest between gigs, find resources, get trained, and connect with contractors to source work. People stay for a nominal fee that keeps the doors open; those who want to stay permanently can, so long as they help care for the community.
how_to_reach_it: Phase 1 - Build the engine: make Deadhang Labor, The Crew Blueprint, Production Atlas, and Contractor Tools into a working Labor to Training to Jobs flow that earns revenue and grows a community of contractors.
Phase 2 - Acquire the ground: secure land once the Phase 1 engine is self-sustaining.
Phase 2 - Build the Homestead: stand up the physical home base and the way-station / stewardship model on that land.
Ongoing - Grow the movement: the engine keeps feeding people, training, and work into the Homestead community.

phases.name: Foundation - Labor, Training, Jobs
phases.goal: Stand up a self-sustaining engine: reliable labor, skill-building, and job discovery that earn revenue and build a contractor community.

phases.name: The Homestead - The Destination
phases.goal: Acquire land and build the physical community home base once the Phase 1 engine can support it.

journey.start_label: Where you are now: a solo contractor earning through Deadhang Labor LLC, building the rest on the side.

milestones.title: Solidify Deadhang's foundation
milestones.summary: Get the business itself in order — legal structure, compliance, and a repeatable way to track gigs — so everything else is built on solid ground.
milestones.outcome: Deadhang is legally sound and every gig is tracked.
milestones.leads_to: A stable base you can safely scale from.

milestones.title: Get paid reliably
milestones.summary: Stand up the day-to-day tools — invoicing, payment tracking, and dependable scheduling — so money and time are never the bottleneck.
milestones.outcome: Invoices, payments, and calendar run without friction.
milestones.leads_to: Operations that hold up as volume grows.

milestones.title: Map the industry (Production Atlas)
milestones.summary: Grow the industry-intelligence platform (not a job board): a static, research-package-driven map of employers, vendors, departments, IATSE pathways, and geographic opportunity. Architecture and the public-safe data model are settled; the work now is research-corpus depth and usability.
milestones.outcome: A trustworthy, searchable map of the industry contractors can act on.
milestones.leads_to: A steady inflow of work to feed people into.

milestones.title: Teach the skills (The Crew Blueprint)
milestones.summary: Grow the training resource content-first: canonical course packets and a visual, workflow-based way for stagehands to learn the craft and how it fits the bigger picture. The authority/safety model is set; the next-gen platform is deferred until content justifies it.
milestones.outcome: People can level up their stagehand skills through the Blueprint.

milestones.title: Build teams & win bigger contracts
milestones.summary: Grow Deadhang from solo work to leading crews — recruit a contractor network and the coordination to deploy teams on larger contracts.
milestones.outcome: Deadhang can staff and lead multi-person jobs.
milestones.leads_to: The labor engine that powers the whole system.

milestones.title: Close the loop — the engine sustains itself
milestones.summary: Connect labor, training, and jobs into one self-reinforcing flywheel: work is sourced, people are trained, teams are deployed, and revenue recycles back in.
milestones.outcome: Labor → Training → Jobs runs as a self-sustaining loop.
milestones.leads_to: Enough stability and revenue to reach for the Homestead.

milestones.title: Acquire the land
milestones.summary: With the engine self-sustaining, secure the ground the Homestead will be built on.
milestones.outcome: Land is secured for the community home base.
milestones.leads_to: A place to build the destination.

milestones.title: Build the Homestead
milestones.summary: Stand up the community home base: a way-station where traveling stagehands rest between gigs, find resources, get trained, and connect to work — with a path for permanent residents who help care for it.
milestones.outcome: The Homestead is open and serving the community.
milestones.leads_to: The movement and the Homestead — the destination.

thirty_sixty_ninety.title: 30 / 60 / 90 Day Operating Dashboard
thirty_sixty_ninety.summary: A near-term execution layer that keeps the roadmap linear while translating the current milestone into practical operating windows.

windows.label: Next 30 days
windows.goal: Close out Deadhang's document finalization and taxes so the administrative base is fully solid.
outcomes: Business documents de-duplicated and consolidated to one canonical set (dh-5)
Tax balance confirmed and payment arranged; returns amended if needed (dh-8)
Contractor agreement + MSA sent for Arizona attorney review (dh-10)
windows.guardrail: Do not engage contractors or scale to team work before the IC agreement is attorney-reviewed and insurance is in place.

windows.label: Next 60 days
windows.goal: Close the remaining reliability gaps in the contractor tool set (invoicing and payment tracking are already built).
outcomes: Backend scope validated against real MVP needs — no overbuilding (ct-1)
Calendar authorization renews reliably without manual refresh (ct-3)
Invoice generator + payment tracking confirmed stable (ct-2, ct-4 already done)
windows.guardrail: Keep tools narrow and reliability-first: don't add backend/enterprise features before the real requirement is validated.

windows.label: Next 90 days
windows.theme: Connect labor, training, and job discovery
windows.goal: Prepare the first usable loop between Deadhang Labor, Crew Blueprint, Production Atlas, and Contractor Tools.
outcomes: Production Atlas deepens its research corpus (Scenic + employer/opportunity datasets)
Crew Blueprint grows its canonical course packets and discipline/pathway library
Anti-sprawl controls keep skipped work visible without breaking the linear path
windows.guardrail: Do not turn the roadmap into a storage dump. Promote only public-safe, decision-ready work.
